engagementShape: advisory engagement (small) — early-stage scoping, not implementation yet
introContext: Saw the Seev booth on the AECTech 2026 floor; Spenser has no specific contact and no prior conversation.
callObjective: Find out what Seev actually does end-to-end (the public marketing is thin), and whether there is a real AI roadmap that an Arvaya advisory engagement could help scope.
hypothesis: Seev is a small early-stage company with a focused product around design/construction process visibility. Their AI story is likely thin — either they're using off-the-shelf models for one feature, or they don't have an AI product yet but see a need. The advisory engagement Arvaya could pitch is small in scope; the right framing is helping them figure out where AI buys them defensibility.
meetingContext: Tier C target on the AEC vendor list. Public footprint is thin — expect the SourcePack to come back light and the brief to invoke the soft-minimum (src-meta-thin) path. The AE wants to walk in knowing whatever the public web does say about them.
competitiveContext: broader AEC platforms (Procore, Autodesk Construction Cloud) absorbing similar visibility features
